heading: Duties of the Commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission, through the authority of the members referred to in , shall take such actions as it deems necessary to streamline, improve, or augment the financial literacy and education programs, grants, and materials of the Federal Government, including curricula for all Americans.section 9702(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: create household budgets, initiate savings plans, and make strategic investment decisions for education, retirement, home ownership, wealth building, or other savings goals;

children.label: B
children.text: manage spending, credit, and debt, including credit card debt, effectively;

children.label: C
children.text: increase awareness of the availability and significance of credit reports and credit scores in obtaining credit, the importance of their accuracy (and how to correct inaccuracies), their effect on credit terms, and the effect common financial decisions may have on credit scores;

children.label: D
children.text: ascertain fair and favorable credit terms;

children.label: E
children.text: avoid abusive, predatory, or deceptive credit offers and financial products;

children.label: F
children.text: understand, evaluate, and compare financial products, services, and opportunities;

children.label: G
children.text: understand resources that ought to be easily accessible and affordable, and that inform and educate investors as to their rights and avenues of recourse when an investor believes his or her rights have been violated by unprofessional conduct of market intermediaries;

children.label: H
children.text: increase awareness of the particular financial needs and financial transactions (such as the sending of remittances) of consumers who are targeted in multilingual financial literacy and education programs and improve the development and distribution of multilingual financial literacy and education materials;

children.label: I
children.text: promote bringing individuals who lack basic banking services into the financial mainstream by opening and maintaining an account with a financial institution; and

children.label: J
children.text: improve financial literacy and education through all other related skills, including personal finance and related economic education, with the primary goal of programs not simply to improve knowledge, but rather to improve consumers’ financial choices and outcomes.

children.heading: Best practices for teaching financial literacy
children.label: 3 Best practices for teaching financial literacy
i
children.text: teach financial literacy skills; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide useful and necessary information to assist students at institutions of higher education when making financial decisions related to student borrowing.

children.label: i
children.text: Methods to ensure that each student has a clear sense of the student’s total borrowing obligations, including monthly payments, and repayment options.

children.label: ii
children.text: The most effective ways to engage students in financial literacy education, including frequency and timing of communication with students.

children.label: iii
children.text: Information on how to target different student populations, including part-time students, first-time students, and other nontraditional students.

children.label: iv
children.text: Ways to clearly communicate the importance of graduating on a student’s ability to repay student loans.

children.heading: Maintenance of best practices
children.label: C Maintenance of best practices
children.text: The Commission shall maintain and periodically update the best practices information required under this paragraph and make the best practices available to the public.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: D Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to require an institution of higher education to adopt the best practices required under this paragraph.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall establish and maintain a website, such as the domain name “FinancialLiteracy.gov”, or a similar domain name.

children.label: A
children.text: serve as a clearinghouse of information about Federal financial literacy and education programs;

children.label: B
children.text: provide a coordinated entry point for accessing information about all Federal publications, grants, and materials promoting enhanced financial literacy and education;

children.label: C
children.text: offer information on all Federal grants to promote financial literacy and education, and on how to target, apply for, and receive a grant that is most appropriate under the circumstances;

children.label: D
children.text: as the Commission considers appropriate, feature website links to efforts that have no commercial content and that feature information about financial literacy and education programs, materials, or campaigns; and

children.label: E
children.text: offer such other information as the Commission finds appropriate to share with the public in the fulfillment of its purpose.

children.heading: Toll-free hotline
children.label: c Toll-free hotline
children.text: The Commission shall establish a toll-free telephone number that shall be made available to members of the public seeking information about issues pertaining to financial literacy and education.

children.heading: Development and dissemination of materials
children.label: d Development and dissemination of materials

children.text: develop materials to promote financial literacy and education; and
disseminate such materials to the general public.

children.heading: Coordination of efforts
children.label: e Coordination of efforts
children.text: The Commission shall take such steps as are necessary to coordinate and promote financial literacy and education efforts at the State and local level, including promoting partnerships among Federal, State, and local governments, nonprofit organizations, and private enterprises.

children.label: A
children.text: not later than 18 months after , develop a national strategy to promote basic financial literacy and education among all American consumers; andDecember 4, 2003

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate Federal efforts to implement the strategy developed under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: participation by State and local governments and private, nonprofit, and public institutions in the creation and implementation of such strategy;

children.label: i
children.text: to increase the general financial education level of current and future consumers of financial services and products; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to enhance the general understanding of financial services and products;

children.label: C
children.text: review of Federal activities designed to promote financial literacy and education, and development of a plan to improve coordination of such activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: the identification of areas of overlap and duplication among Federal financial literacy and education activities and proposed means of eliminating any such overlap and duplication.

children.heading: National strategy review
children.label: 3 National strategy review
children.text: The Commission shall, not less than annually, review the national strategy developed under this subsection and make such changes and recommendations as it deems necessary.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: g Consultation
children.text: The Commission shall actively consult with a variety of representatives from private and nonprofit organizations and State and local agencies, as determined appropriate by the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of the first meeting of the Commission, and annually thereafter, the Commission shall issue a report, the Strategy for Assuring Financial Empowerment (“SAFE Strategy”), to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives on the progress of the Commission in carrying out this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: the national strategy for financial literacy and education, as described under subsection (f);

children.label: B
children.text: information concerning the implementation of the duties of the Commission under subsections (a) through (g);

children.label: C
children.text: an assessment of the success of the Commission in implementing the national strategy developed under subsection (f);

children.label: D
children.text: an assessment of the availability, utilization, and impact of Federal financial literacy and education materials;

children.label: i
children.text: the website established under subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the toll-free telephone number established under subsection (c);

children.label: F
children.text: a brief survey of the financial literacy and education materials developed under subsection (d), and data regarding the dissemination and impact of such materials, as measured by improved financial decisionmaking;

children.label: G
children.text: a brief summary of any hearings conducted by the Commission, including a list of witnesses who testified at such hearings;

children.label: H
children.text: information about the activities of the Commission planned for the next fiscal year;

children.label: I
children.text: a summary of all Federal financial literacy and education activities targeted to communities that have historically lacked access to financial literacy materials and education, and have been underserved by the mainstream financial systems; and

children.label: J
children.text: such other materials relating to the duties of the Commission as the Commission deems appropriate.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: 3 Initial report
children.text: The initial report under paragraph (1) shall include information regarding all Federal programs, materials, and grants which seek to improve financial literacy, and assess the effectiveness of such programs.

children.heading: Testimony
children.label: i Testimony
children.text: The Commission shall annually provide testimony by the Chairperson to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives.
